title: Today's AI Brief: Anthropic has officially filed to go public
summary: Anthropic files to go public, Endava uses Codex to accelerate software delivery
imageAlt: Image from theverge.com for Anthropic has officially filed to go public
imageCaption: Anthropic has officially filed to go public (theverge.com)

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your AI briefing. I've got a few updates to share with you today that are making waves in the AI space. It's always exciting to see how AI is evolving, and today's news has some interesting implications.

First up, Anthropic has officially filed to go public. This move is interesting because it puts Anthropic in the same arena as OpenAI, another major player in the AI field. The competition between these two companies is heating up, and going public could give Anthropic the resources it needs to stay in the race. I'm curious to see how this will play out - will Anthropic's IPO be a game-changer for the AI industry?

Next, I'm looking at how Endava is using Codex to build what they call an 'agentic organization'. Essentially, they're using AI to speed up software delivery and cut down on the time it takes to analyze requirements - from weeks to just hours. It's a practical example of how AI can transform the way companies work. I've always been fascinated by the potential for AI to streamline processes, and Endava's approach is a great example of this in action.

How Endava builds an agentic organization with Codex is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: Learn how Endava uses Codex to build an agentic organization, accelerating software delivery and reducing requirements analysis from weeks to hours. The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
So that is the brief version: a few headlines, a little context, and just enough connective tissue to make the day easier to follow without turning the whole thing into homework.
